product.name: MirrorTrace
product.subtitle: Self Footprint Audit Pro
product.oneLiner: Map what the public web reveals about you, turn findings into actions, and clear the trace when you are done.

inversion.headline: A private, action-oriented view of your public exposure.
inversion.body: MirrorTrace turns public-source OSINT into a self-audit: map your own footprint, preserve citable evidence involving you, monitor authorized public entities, and prepare practical remediation.
points: Scan your own, authorized, or public-entity surfaces
Preserve citable public evidence with source and timestamp
Link exposure sources through shared identifiers
Prepare opt-out, takedown, and paced recheck actions
Keep report data session-local and purgeable

scopeTypes.label: self · my own footprint
scopeTypes.desc: Audit your own public digital footprint.
scopeTypes.example: Search public pages for appearances of my own name.

scopeTypes.label: consented · authorized subject
scopeTypes.desc: The subject has authorized you in writing to audit their public footprint.
scopeTypes.example: Audit public mentions of a client brand under written authorization.

scopeTypes.label: public_figure · public figure
scopeTypes.desc: Public statements and reporting about officials or public figures.
scopeTypes.example: Summarize public news coverage and official statements from a political figure.

scopeTypes.label: brand · brand / organization
scopeTypes.desc: Monitor public reputation sources for a company, product, or organization.
scopeTypes.example: Monitor public news and official pages for a brand recall.

scopeTypes.label: safety_evidence · safety evidence
scopeTypes.desc: Preserve public evidence involving you (harassment, defamation, or fraud).
scopeTypes.example: Preserve a public defamatory post targeting me as evidence.

prohibited.label: Infer gender or sexual orientation from an avatar / image
Detect whether someone is active on a dating app

nodes.title: A0 · Policy Gate
nodes.role: Policy gate. Every request starts here: parse scope_type, match the input_schema enum, refuse prohibited patterns, and return safer alternatives. This is the pipeline's only entrance: compliance as code.

nodes.title: A2 · Source Router (Metamorph)
nodes.role: Route compliant requests to public-source actors for search engines, news, official sites, or public archives. Use Apify Metamorph to switch actors dynamically instead of hard-coding a monolith.

nodes.title: A3 · Adaptive Crawler
nodes.role: AdaptivePlaywrightCrawler uses plain HTTP where possible and upgrades to a browser only when rendering is needed. It fetches public pages only and stops at login walls, CAPTCHAs, or blocks.

nodes.title: A5 · Evidence & Scoring
nodes.role: Structure public evidence, compute exposure / evidence_quality / actionability / distress_risk scores, and build a citable evidence index with URL, timestamp, and hash.

nodes.title: A6 · Report & Remediation
nodes.role: Generate a self-footprint report centered on the Exposure Map, with broker opt-outs, GDPR/CCPA takedown requests, and paced rechecks.

webhooks.title: Webhook · audit.completed
webhooks.role: Audit-complete callback: push the report structure and evidence index to the user's own endpoint, not a third party.

webhooks.title: Webhook · policy.rejected
webhooks.role: Policy-refusal callback: record refusal metadata and reasons for compliance auditing without storing rejected subject content.

cards.title: Metamorph dynamic routing
cards.what: Use Apify Actor.metamorph() to transform the current run into the next actor at runtime, routing by scope_type without a giant hard-coded actor.
cards.bonus: Compliance advantage: the routing table is itself an allowlist. Only declared public-source actors are reachable.

cards.title: MCP tool-layer allowlist
cards.what: Wrap collection and retrieval capabilities as MCP tools. The model can call only explicitly exposed tools, each carrying scope constraints.
cards.bonus: Compliance advantage: the tool layer is a hard boundary. Even a manipulated model cannot call a private-person tracking tool that does not exist.

cards.title: input_schema enum = compliance
cards.what: The Actor input_schema defines scope_type as a fixed enum (self / consented / public_figure / brand / safety_evidence). Values outside the enum fail during input validation.
cards.bonus: Compliance advantage: compliance as code. The platform enforces the schema rather than relying on a runtime if statement or operator discipline.

cards.title: AdaptivePlaywrightCrawler
cards.what: Crawlee adaptive crawling starts with lightweight HTTP and upgrades to Playwright only when JavaScript rendering is required.
cards.bonus: Compliance advantage: handle public renderable pages only, minimizing both cost and fingerprint surface instead of over-simulating a human.

cards.title: Compliant backoff, not evasion
cards.what: On 429, blocks, or CAPTCHA, back off and eventually stop. Record the state honestly. Never rotate IPs, spoof fingerprints, or defeat CAPTCHAs.
cards.bonus: Compliance advantage: backing off respects the other service's boundary. Evasion crosses it. MirrorTrace explicitly chooses backoff.

report.label: Example shape (template, not live crawl data)

scores.label: Exposure
scores.desc: How broadly your public footprint can be retrieved (0-100).

scores.label: Evidence quality
scores.desc: How citable preserved evidence is: source authority + timestamp + integrity (0-100).

scores.label: Actionability
scores.desc: How many findings include a clear next step such as removal request, report, or preservation (0-100).

scores.label: Distress risk
scores.desc: Risk that the content causes distress to you, used for ranking and reminders (backend-scored, 0-100).

evidenceIndexFields.desc: Permitted scope for this evidence row
Evidence subject (self / authorized / public figure / brand only)
Suggested action (preserve / request removal / report to platform / seek legal advice)

plan48h.title: Policy Gate + input_schema
plan48h.desc: Implement the A0 policy gate and scope_type enum with refusal, acceptance, and safer-alternative logic.

plan48h.title: Metamorph routing + allowlisted sources
plan48h.desc: Wire the A2 routing table to allowlisted public-source actors and expose only the MCP tool-layer allowlist.

plan48h.title: Adaptive collection + backoff
plan48h.desc: A3 adaptive crawler, compliant backoff, and public-page boundary.

plan48h.title: Scoring + evidence index
plan48h.desc: A5 four-score model and structured evidence index.

plan48h.title: Report + remediation + demo
plan48h.desc: A6 report generation, Exposure Map, opt-out / takedown remediation, and demo polish.
